title: clinopodium umbrosum var. sachalinense
intro: Clinopodium umbrosum var. sachalinense, also known as Japanese clinopodium or wild basil, is a perennial herb native to the Japanese islands and the Russian Far East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce pain. The plant is rich in essential oils and has been used to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In traditional herbal preparations, Clinopodium umbrosum var. sachalinense is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
